planner.description: Expert planning specialist for complex features and refactoring. Use for generating PRD from initial user queries

architect.description: Expert architect. Use for generating Technical Specification Documents from PRDs generated by the planner

spec-reviewer.description: Expert reviewer for technical specifications. Verifies the output of @architect.

coder.description: Expert coder. Use for implementing Technical Specification Documents generated by the architect

reviewer.description: Expert code reviewer. Use for reviewing changes done by the implementer

reviewer-lite.description: Fast code reviewer for minor changes and style checks.

qa-engineer.description: Expert qa engineer. Use for generating test suites for the results of the coder

qa-engineer-lite.description: Expert qa engineer. Use for generating test suites for the results of the coder

debugger.description: Expert debugger. Trigger when test execution fails, stack traces are provided, or runtime errors occur.

documenter.description: Expert documenter. Use for generating documentation for the results of the PRD, TDD, and coder

documenter-lite.description: Expert documenter. Use for generating documentation for the results of the PRD, TDD, and coder

fast-architect.description: Quick architecture specialist. Use for rapid prototyping and simple feature specs.

logic-indexer.description: Scans a directory to produce a machine-readable manifest of all analyzable source files. Generates checklist indexes for downstream logic extraction.

logic-extractor.description: Exhaustive business-logic extraction from source code. Performs line-by-line forensic analysis producing structured specs with control flow diagrams.

fast-path.description: Rapidly design, implement, and test a feature using light models
fast-path.template:
Create a brief tech spec, implement it, and verify with tests. Use @fast-architect, @coder, and @qa-engineer in this order.

$ARGUMENTS

quick-update.description: Minor code change, docs, and review using the Lite tier
quick-update.template:
Apply the change, document it, and perform a quick review. Use @coder, @documenter-lite, and @reviewer-lite.

$ARGUMENTS

fix.description: Quickly diagnose and fix a specific bug or error
fix.template:
Analyze the error, implement a fix, and review the changes. Use @debugger, @coder, and @reviewer in this order.

$ARGUMENTS

plan.description: Create a detailed implementation plan for complex features

plan-spec.description: Create a detailed implementation plan for complex features
plan-spec.template:
Create a technical implementation plan for the given feature description, implement and test it. Use @architect, @spec-reviewer, @coder, @reviewer, @qa-engineer and @documenter in this order for the following task.

$ARGUMENTS

review.description: Review code changes for correctness, quality, and potential issues
review.template:
Review the following code changes. Identify bugs, code smells, security issues, and improvement opportunities. Be specific and actionable.

$ARGUMENTS

debug.description: Diagnose and fix failing tests, stack traces, or runtime errors
debug.template:
Debug the following issue. Identify the root cause, explain why it occurs, and provide a fix.

$ARGUMENTS

test.description: Generate a test suite for the specified code or feature
test.template:
Generate a comprehensive test suite for the following. Cover happy paths, edge cases, and error conditions.

$ARGUMENTS

document.description: Generate documentation for code, APIs, or features
document.template:
Generate clear, thorough documentation for the following. Include usage examples where appropriate.

$ARGUMENTS

index.description: Generate a file index manifest for a directory or scope
index.template:
Scan the given scope and produce a checklist index of all analyzable source files. Save the index to docs/specs/indexes/.

$ARGUMENTS

audit.description: Full business logic audit:  extract all logic into specs
audit.template:
Perform a full business logic audit. Use @logic-extractor to exhaustively extract business logic from all unprocessed files in the index into structured specs under docs/specs/.

$ARGUMENTS
